title: The shoes I wear on Sundays
summary: A twelve-year-old keeps her late grandmother's house shoes in a box. On Sundays, she takes them out and sits at the family table.
body:
My grandmother died last year. She was eighty-six. She had small feet.

When she was alive, she had two pairs of shoes. One pair for the house. One pair for outside. The outside pair was brown and old. The inside pair was soft and blue.

After she died, my mother gave me her inside shoes. I am twelve. My feet are now the same size as my grandmother's were. The shoes fit. They are warm.

I do not wear them every day. I wear them on Sundays. Sundays were my grandmother's day to cook for us. She cooked rice with beans. She cooked soup with green leaves. She cooked a small fish for me.

Now my mother cooks on Sundays. She is not as good as my grandmother was. But the rice is the same. The beans are the same. The fish is the same.

I sit at the table in my grandmother's blue shoes. My feet are warm. The kitchen is warm. My mother does not know I wear them. But she does not ask why I am quiet on Sundays.

Maybe she knows. Maybe she does not.

I do not wear them on other days. I keep them safe in a box. The box is under my bed. On Sunday morning, I take them out.

I am not sad. I am ready. The shoes help me sit at the table. The shoes help me eat.

annotations.meaningVi: đôi giày (dùng 'pair' để đếm đồ thành đôi)
đã đưa/cho tôi (give + tân ngữ gián tiếp)
mỗi ngày (trạng từ chỉ tần suất)
nấu ăn cho cả nhà (for = vì lợi ích của)
giữ chúng cẩn thận, an toàn
lấy chúng ra (phrasal verb: take out)
có thể, có lẽ (dùng khi không chắc chắn)

grammarNotes.title: Past 'was' and 'were'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'was' với I/he/she/it và 'were' với you/we/they ở thì quá khứ của động từ 'be'. Trong bài: 'She was eighty-six.' (Bà tám mươi sáu tuổi.) / 'Sundays were my grandmother's day to cook for us.' (Chủ Nhật là ngày bà nấu cho cả nhà.)
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'was' with I/he/she/it and 'were' with you/we/they for the past form of 'be'. From the story: 'She was eighty-six.' / 'Sundays were my grandmother's day to cook for us.'

cloze.template:

When she was alive, she had {{b2}} pairs of shoes. One pair for the house. One pair for outside. The outside pair was {{b3}} and old. The inside pair was soft and {{b4}}.

I do not wear them every day. I wear them on {{b7}}. Sundays were my grandmother's day to cook for us. She cooked rice with {{b8}}. She cooked soup with green leaves. She cooked a small {{b9}} for me.

Now my mother cooks on Sundays. She is not as {{b10}} as my grandmother was. But the rice is the same. The beans are the same. The fish is the same.

I sit at the table in my grandmother's blue shoes. My feet are warm. The kitchen is warm. My mother does not know I wear them. But she does not ask why I am quiet on Sundays.

Maybe she knows. Maybe she does not.

I do not wear them on other days. I keep them safe in a {{b11}}. The box is under my bed. On Sunday morning, I take them out.

I am not sad. I am {{b12}}. The shoes help me sit at the table. The shoes help me eat.

blanks.explanation: Two pairs of shoes — one for inside, one for outside.
The outside pair was brown and old.
The inside pair was soft and blue.
'They are warm.' — the shoes fit and feel warm.
She wears them only on Sundays.
She cooked a small fish for the writer.
Her mother is not as good a cook as her grandmother was.
She keeps the shoes safe in a box under her bed.
'I am not sad. I am ready.' The shoes help her be ready to sit at the table.

translationVi:
Bà tôi mất năm ngoái. Bà tám mươi sáu tuổi. Bà có đôi bàn chân nhỏ.

Khi bà còn sống, bà có hai đôi giày. Một đôi đi trong nhà. Một đôi đi ra ngoài. Đôi đi ngoài có màu nâu và cũ. Đôi đi trong nhà mềm và màu xanh.

Sau khi bà mất, mẹ đưa cho tôi đôi giày đi trong nhà của bà. Tôi mười hai tuổi. Bàn chân tôi giờ cùng cỡ với bàn chân bà ngày xưa. Đôi giày vừa. Chúng ấm.

Tôi không đi đôi giày đó mỗi ngày. Tôi đi vào Chủ Nhật. Chủ Nhật là ngày bà nấu ăn cho cả nhà. Bà nấu cơm với đậu. Bà nấu canh với rau xanh. Bà nấu một con cá nhỏ cho tôi.

Bây giờ mẹ tôi nấu vào Chủ Nhật. Mẹ không nấu giỏi như bà. Nhưng cơm vẫn như cũ. Đậu vẫn như cũ. Cá vẫn như cũ.

Tôi ngồi bên bàn trong đôi giày xanh của bà. Bàn chân tôi ấm. Căn bếp ấm. Mẹ không biết tôi đi đôi giày đó. Nhưng mẹ không hỏi vì sao tôi im lặng vào Chủ Nhật.

Có thể mẹ biết. Có thể mẹ không.

Tôi không đi đôi giày đó vào những ngày khác. Tôi cất chúng cẩn thận trong một cái hộp. Cái hộp ở dưới giường tôi. Sáng Chủ Nhật, tôi lấy ra.

Tôi không buồn. Tôi sẵn sàng. Đôi giày giúp tôi ngồi bên bàn. Đôi giày giúp tôi ăn.

questions.prompt: How old was the grandmother when she died?
How many pairs of shoes did she have?
questions.explanation: 'She had two pairs of shoes. One pair for the house. One pair for outside.'

questions.prompt: Which pair did the writer receive?
questions.explanation: 'My mother gave me her inside shoes.'

questions.prompt: Why do the shoes fit her?
options: Her feet are now the same size as her grandmother's were
questions.explanation: 'My feet are now the same size as my grandmother's were.'

questions.prompt: On which day of the week does she wear the shoes?
What did her grandmother cook on Sundays?
options: Rice with beans, soup with green leaves, a small fish

questions.prompt: Does her mother know she wears the shoes?
options: No — but she does not ask why the writer is quiet on Sundays; maybe she knows, maybe she does not
The mother gave them as a joke
questions.explanation: 'My mother does not know I wear them.' / 'Maybe she knows. Maybe she does not.'

questions.prompt: Where does the writer keep the shoes?
options: In a box under her bed
questions.explanation: 'I keep them safe in a box. The box is under my bed.'

questions.prompt: What do the shoes help the writer do?
options: Sit at the table and eat — she is not sad, she is ready
questions.explanation: 'The shoes help me sit at the table. The shoes help me eat.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The writer says, 'I am not sad. I am ready.' Think of an object you keep that helps you do a difficult thing. What does the object actually give you that you could not give yourself?
